project.title: PARQ Integration to One Bangkok Application
project.overview: A repository-backed project hub for the PARQ integration workstream. It organizes source-of-truth documents, discovery notes, architecture artifacts, delivery references, QA inputs, open decisions, and stakeholder navigation.
project.businessContext: PARQ integration work connects PARQ-related user flows, identity, workplace, visitor, parking, notification, and supporting systems into the One Bangkok application context. The portal summarizes existing repository artifacts only; it does not approve or create requirements.
project.why: The hub exists so Product, Design, Development, QA, vendors, and management can quickly understand current status, blockers, next actions, and role-relevant source artifacts without reading every markdown file first.
project.statusNote: PARQ-REQ-001 v0.4 is accepted for internal estimation. Simon is recommended next for technical estimation alignment / TDD gap check. Quinn remains waiting until PARQ explicitly hands off QA readiness work; detailed QA scenarios are not automatically started.

readinessCards.label: Source of Truth

readinessCards.detail: Simon is next for technical estimation alignment / TDD gap check against the accepted Requirement/SOW baseline.

readinessCards.label: QA
readinessCards.detail: Quinn remains waiting until Simon completes technical estimation alignment / TDD gap check and PARQ explicitly hands off QA readiness work.

keyDocuments.name: PARQ Architecture Readiness Reassessment After Owner Confirmation
keyDocuments.status: Draft / Ready for QA Readiness Planning with Blockers
keyDocuments.notes: Confirms QA readiness/environment dependency planning can start at register level. Final SIT/UAT execution planning remains blocked by named contacts, endpoint contracts, environments, test data, timeout/fallback rules, audit/reconciliation, and hardware details.

keyDocuments.name: PARQ Architecture Impact Assessment From Molly UX Update
keyDocuments.status: Draft / Developer Review Go with Blockers
keyDocuments.downstream: Developer technical reviewers, Quinn, PARQ, Libra, Molly, Simon
keyDocuments.notes: Assesses Molly UX update impact across Parking Availability, Parking Ticket/Payment, Notification, Multi-Tower, CMS, Account Lifecycle, and BMS. Developer technical review can start with blockers; BMS Option B remains valid.

keyDocuments.name: PARQ Requirement Specification and Scope of Work v0.4
keyDocuments.type: Requirement Specification / Scope of Work baseline
keyDocuments.downstream: Simon, PARQ, internal estimation team, Quinn after explicit handoff
keyDocuments.notes: Bas/PARQ accepted v0.4 as the internal baseline for estimation preparation. Acceptance does not start detailed QA scenarios automatically; Simon is next for technical estimation alignment / TDD gap check.
